extraction.one_line_summary: STORER Michael won from 30-rider breakaway while GROSSCHARTNER Felix missed GC lead by 8 seconds through conservative cat-and-mouse tactics in final group.
tactical_patterns: DSM committed five of nine riders to breakaway, overwhelming numerical advantage enabled STORER's stage win with teammates controlling tempo.
GROSSCHARTNER Felix lost red jersey by 8 seconds by playing cat-and-mouse in chase group instead of attacking hard.
Movistar lost tactical advantage of three riders in top-five after VALVERDE crashed, neutralizing their numerical superiority over ROGLIC.
YATES Adam rode aggressively for BERNAL despite losing time stage 2, suggesting Ineos prioritizing BERNAL over YATES for GC.

rider_signals.STORER Michael: positive: Won from major breakaway with DSM team support, confirming aggressive climbing form.
rider_signals.GROSSCHARTNER Felix: negative: Miscalculated final chase group dynamics, lost red jersey by 8 seconds through passive tactics.
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: negative: High-speed crash eliminated him, destroying Movistar's 3-in-top-5 numerical advantage over ROGLIC.
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: negative: Dropped badly on Balcon de Alicante, already 1:12 down confirms poor GC form.
rider_signals.CARUSO Damiano: negative: Dropped long before finish on final climb, Bahrain GC ambitions collapsing early.
rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: Setting hard tempo for BERNAL despite own GC losses, strong climbing legs shown.
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: Best time from chase group on final climb, strongest domestique performance.
